danger_signals.visual_form: Terminator and Skynet robot sprites with metallic/industrial design
Dystopian 2029 setting with industrial ruins, metal structures, and destroyed cityscape
danger_signals.meaning: High-danger world state; post-apocalyptic threat environment

danger_signals.visual_form: Police stations, nightclub environments, crowded civilian areas
danger_signals.meaning: Danger from Terminator infiltration in human society; collateral risk

safety_signals.meaning: Player progression points (though noted as spaced too far apart, creating difficulty spikes)
Human resistance against machines; player agency in preventing Judgment Day

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Terminator machine sprites with varying designs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Future war 2029 industrial ruins; dystopian metal structures; destroyed Los Angeles
Police station interiors; nightclub setting (1984 Los Angeles); contemporary urban environments

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dystopian industrial landscape with ruins, metal structures, destroyed cityscape, post-apocalyptic aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Machine enemies actively hunting; combat-focused gameplay; no civilian NPCs present

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Contemporary urban environments including police stations and nightclubs; civilian infrastructure intact
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Terminator infiltration requiring stealth/evasion through human spaces; civilian presence creates environmental complexity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Barren landscapes, industrial ruins, mechanical enemy prevalence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must navigate hostile machine patrols; no safe zones except checkpoints

environmental_storytelling.example: Level design transitions from 2029 future war sequences to 1984 Los Angeles, visually communicating timeline and narrative context
Industrial, dystopian ruins represent Skynet dominance; intact urban structures (police station, nightclub) represent pre-Judgment Day human civilization
Driving levels break up platforming, suggesting different navigation challenges in different temporal/spatial contexts

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Sprite movement patterns suggest relentless pursuit; enemy AI design reflects mechanical nature
Platforming actions and evasion mechanics reflect human vulnerability against superior machine force

notes: Source material is primarily a Wikipedia index page listing game releases without detailed visual/mechanical analysis. Graphics were criticized as resembling 8-bit rather than 16-bit SNES capability, with 'dreary' aesthetic noted by reviewers. Game structure includes platforming and driving stages across two primary settings: 2029 future war and 1984 Los Angeles. Limited documentation prevents detailed faction visual identity extraction beyond thematic elements.
sources_used: wikipedia: List of Terminator video games - Terminator 2: Judgment Day (1991 film) games section, Nintendo versions subsection
